TSLA: Unveiling the Mystery Behind Elon Musk's Exploration of the Photovoltaic Industry Chain. Elon Musk's teams (SpaceX and Tesla) have reportedly "surveyed" China's photovoltaic industry, showing particular interest in heterojunction and perovskite technologies for both terrestrial AI data centers and ambitious space solar power plans. This move has spurred significant market reaction in China's space photovoltaic sector, with companies like JinkoSolar confirming contact with Musk's inspection teams. The initiative highlights a potential new era for Chinese PV enterprises, moving towards higher technological thresholds required for space applications, and could revolutionize the global energy landscape by leveraging solar power in space.

Tesla's China-made EV sales rise 9.3% y/y in January. Tesla sold 69,129 China-made electric vehicles in January, marking a 9.3% increase year-over-year and extending gains for a third consecutive month despite battling shrinking market share in China and Europe. Deliveries of its Model 3 and Model Y from the Shanghai plant, including exports, dropped 28.9% from December. The company rolled out a seven-year, low-interest financing plan in China, prompting rivals to offer similar incentives.

CarMax Named One of TIME’s America’s Most Iconic Companies. CarMax has been recognized by TIME Magazine as one of America's Most Iconic Companies, a project created in partnership with Statista Inc. This honor acknowledges CarMax's long-standing dedication to providing a transparent and customer-centric experience in the used car market, marking it as an enduring symbol of American business, culture, and identity. The recognition highlights CarMax's innovation, "no-haggle" pricing, and commitment to empowering customers, placing it alongside other automotive giants like Ford, General Motors, and Tesla.

Tesla Ending Production Of Model S & Model Y. Tesla will cease production of its Model S sedan and Model X SUV in the second quarter of 2026, shifting resources to robotics, AI, and autonomous vehicle development. CEO Elon Musk confirmed this decision, stating that the Fremont factory space will be repurposed for Optimus humanoid robot production. This move reflects a strategic pivot for Tesla, as the Model 3 and Model Y have become its volume leaders and the company faces financial headwinds and increased competition in the EV market.

Chinese solar shares surge after reports of Tesla visits. Chinese solar shares surged after local media reported that Tesla staff visited several photovoltaic companies in China. This comes days after Elon Musk announced plans for large-scale solar cell production in the U.S. and highlighted the "underestimated" solar opportunity, aiming for 100 gigawatts of solar cell production annually.

Tesla sets battery storage deployment record in Q4 as EV sales slump. Tesla achieved record-setting stationary battery deployments in Q4 2025, reaching 14.2 GWh, and 46.7 GWh for the full year, despite a significant slowdown in electric vehicle sales. The company's energy business saw robust demand for both Powerwall and Megapack products, leading to record profit margins for the fifth consecutive quarter. Amidst these developments, Tesla plans to expand its Megapack production and CEO Elon Musk emphasized a strong future for the energy segment, including a significant investment in solar PV production.

New Tesla Model Y Trim Drops Price of AWD $7,000. Tesla has introduced a new entry-level all-wheel-drive Model Y trim for the 2026 model year, reducing the AWD starting price by $7,000. While offering a lower price point and maintaining the 0-60 mph time of the Premium trim, this new variant comes with compromises including a shorter range, slower charging, and the removal of various features like a panoramic moonroof, certain interior finishes, and advanced climate control systems. Despite these deletions, key safety features, heated front seats, and the option for Full Self Driving remain.

Gavin Newsom Touts $200 Million EV Incentive Program For First-Time Buyers In California— What It Means For Tesla, Ford, Rivian And GM. California Governor Gavin Newsom has proposed a $200 million EV incentive program targeting first-time buyers through point-of-sale rebates. Participating automakers, including Tesla, Ford, GM, and Rivian, would need to match the incentive amount. This program, which includes incentives for both new and used EVs, follows the elimination of the $7,500 Federal EV Credit and aims to boost EV accessibility in California.
TGT: Target to hand out $1,711.93 checks thanks to $2.2m settlement. Target is distributing $1,711.93 checks from a $2.2 million settlement to individuals who applied for jobs in Washington State between January 1, 2023, and July 26, 2025, where the job postings lacked wage scales, salary ranges, or benefits information. The settlement addresses allegations that Target violated Washington’s Equal Pay and Opportunities Act. Eligible class members must submit a claim form by March 31, 2026, to receive their share of the fund.

3 Consumer Dividend Stocks to Buy for High-Yield Dividend Growth. This article highlights three consumer dividend stocks—Realty Income, Target, and Clorox—that offer high yields and consistent dividend growth. Realty Income is a monthly dividend REIT with a 5.3% yield, benefiting from recent interest rate reductions. Target, a Dividend King with a 4.3% yield, is undergoing a revitalization strategy, and Clorox, yielding 4.4%, is expected to improve performance after addressing past challenges.

What's taking Kroger so long to name a new CEO?. Kroger has been searching for a new CEO for nearly a year following its previous CEO's abrupt resignation, a process that analysts deem to be dragging. This prolonged search contrasts sharply with the smooth leadership transitions seen at rival retailers like Walmart and Target. The company is actively working with a national search firm to find an external candidate, aiming for a "fresh perspective" and expecting to announce a pick in the first quarter of 2026.

Dollar General Bets on Remodels as Its Next Growth Engine. Dollar General is shifting its growth strategy from new store openings to a strong focus on store remodels, particularly through its Project Elevate and Project Renovate programs, due to their attractive return profiles and lower capital intensity. The company plans for remodels to account for about 90% of its fiscal 2026 real estate projects alongside fewer new store openings, aiming to boost productivity and customer satisfaction in existing locations. Despite this shift, Dollar General's shares have outperformed its industry and competitors like Costco and Target, and it holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy).
SBUX: Ashton Thomas Private Wealth LLC Grows Stock Position in Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. Ashton Thomas Private Wealth LLC significantly increased its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 58.1% in Q3, acquiring an additional 39,395 shares to own a total of 107,215 shares valued at $9.07 million. This move aligns with other major institutions like Vanguard, State Street, and Norges Bank, which also boosted or initiated positions, pushing institutional ownership to 72.29%. Despite missing EPS consensus for the recent quarter, Starbucks reported strong revenue and announced a quarterly dividend, while analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a $104.67 price target.

Trending this week: FAT Brands and Twin Hospitality file for Ch. 11 bankruptcy. FAT Brands and its affiliates, including Twin Hospitality, have filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy due to significant liabilities and liquidity issues. The filing covers numerous brands such as Fazoli’s, Round Table Pizza, and Johnny Rockets. Other trending news includes Starbucks reinventing its chai latte and Torchy’s Tacos exiting the Columbus, Ohio, market.

Starbucks plans hundreds of new stores following closures - Bizwomen. Starbucks plans to open 600 to 650 new stores globally in 2026, including 150-175 in the U.S. and 400-500 internationally, with nearly half in China. This move follows the closure of over 400 locations in 2025 as part of CEO Brian Niccol's "Back to Starbucks" restructuring plan. The company's recent earnings report showed a positive 4% growth in U.S. same-store sales, indicating the strategy is yielding early results.
DIS: Josh D’Amaro named Chief Executive Officer (CEO) at The Walt Disney Company. Josh D'Amaro, previously chairman of Disney Experiences, has been appointed CEO of The Walt Disney Company, succeeding Bob Iger. D'Amaro, a 28-year Disney veteran, will assume his new role on March 18 and will oversee the company's vast entertainment empire. The announcement follows a record $10 billion revenue quarter for Disney's Experiences division, and D'Amaro's significant compensation package details were also disclosed.

Atala Financial Inc Makes New Investment in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Atala Financial Inc has acquired a new stake in The Walt Disney Company, purchasing 18,054 shares valued at approximately $2.07 million, making it their 25th largest holding. This investment comes as Disney reported Q1 earnings that beat estimates, though management indicated softer near-term guidance. The article also details recent analyst ratings, institutional investor activity, and key trending news affecting Disney's stock performance.

Josh D’Amaro Takes the Helm: A New Era for The Walt Disney Company. Josh D’Amaro has been appointed as the new CEO of The Walt Disney Company, concluding a two-year selection process. D'Amaro, previously head of Disney's parks and consumer products, will succeed Bob Iger, with his tenure beginning March 18, 2026. Dana Walden has also been promoted to president and chief creative officer, reporting to D'Amaro, as Disney focuses on a strong succession plan after previous leadership challenges.

The Walt Disney Company announced on Tuesday that Josh D'Amaro will become the company's new CEO, replacing Robert Iger.. The Walt Disney Company announced that Josh D'Amaro has been appointed as the new CEO. He will be replacing the outgoing CEO, Robert Iger. This news was shared on Tuesday via Boston 25 News.

Josh D'Amaro named next Walt Disney Company CEO. The Walt Disney Company has announced that Josh D’Amaro, currently head of the company's Experiences division, will be its next CEO. D'Amaro, 54, is set to replace the outgoing CEO, Bob Iger, who plans to retire later this year.

Why FuboTV Stock Crashed Today. FuboTV's stock (FUBO) crashed 22% after the company announced plans for a reverse stock split, signaling investor concern despite a 40% revenue increase to $1.5 billion in Q1 2026. The reverse split, intended to reduce share count, is often seen by investors as a sign of struggle rather than growth. While a recent deal with Disney's Hulu + Live TV boosted revenue, FuboTV still reported a net loss of $19.1 million for the quarter.

Josh D’Amaro Named Next Chief Executive Officer of The Walt Disney Company. The Walt Disney Company's Board of Directors has unanimously elected Josh D’Amaro, Chairman of Disney Experiences, as the next Chief Executive Officer, effective March 18, 2026, succeeding Robert A. Iger. Concurrently, Dana Walden has been named President and Chief Creative Officer of The Walt Disney Company. This transition follows an extensive succession planning process and aims to continue Disney's strategic growth and innovation.

Josh D’Amaro Named Next CEO of The Walt Disney Company. Josh D’Amaro, Chairman of Disney Experiences, has been unanimously elected by The Walt Disney Company Board of Directors to become the next CEO, succeeding Robert Iger, effective March 18, 2026. Concurrent with this, Dana Walden, Co-Chairman of Disney Entertainment, has been named President and Chief Creative Officer. D'Amaro, a 28-year Disney veteran, has been praised for his leadership, strategic vision, and passion for the Disney brand, while Walden's new role highlights the importance of storytelling and creative expression across the company.

Josh D’Amaro Named Next Chief Executive Officer of The Walt Disney Company. The Walt Disney Company's Board of Directors has unanimously elected Josh D'Amaro, current Chairman of Disney Experiences and a 28-year Disney veteran, as the next Chief Executive Officer, effective March 18, 2026, succeeding Robert A. Iger. Additionally, Dana Walden, Co-Chairman of Disney Entertainment, will become President and Chief Creative Officer of The Walt Disney Company, reporting directly to D’Amaro. These appointments mark a new leadership era for Disney, with Iger transitioning to Senior Advisor until his retirement at the end of 2026.

Josh D’Amaro Named Next CEO of The Walt Disney Company. Josh D’Amaro, currently Chairman of Disney Experiences, has been named the next CEO of The Walt Disney Company, effective March 18, 2026. Concurrent with this, Dana Walden will become President and Chief Creative Officer in a newly created role. Robert A. Iger will transition to Senior Advisor and remain on the Board through 2026, marking the conclusion of a multi-year succession planning process.

Disney names Josh D'Amaro CEO, Dana Walden president and chief creative officer. The Walt Disney Company announced that Josh D'Amaro will succeed Bob Iger as CEO in March 2026, with Dana Walden appointed as president and chief creative officer. D'Amaro, currently chair of Disney’s experiences unit, will officially take over on March 18. Iger expressed confidence in D'Amaro's leadership, highlighting his understanding of the Disney brand and his ability to combine creativity with operational excellence.

Josh D’Amaro Named Next CEO of The Walt Disney Company. The Walt Disney Company has announced that Josh D’Amaro, currently chairman of Disney Experiences, will succeed Bob Iger as CEO, effective March 18, 2026. D'Amaro, a 28-year Disney veteran, leads the company’s largest business segment and is recognized for his leadership and vision. Additionally, Dana Walden has been named president and chief creative officer, reporting directly to D’Amaro, while Iger will remain a senior advisor until the end of 2026.

FUBO reverse stock split: FuboTV makes a rare move, streamer’s share price plunges 25%. FuboTV Inc. shares plummeted 25% after the company announced its Q1 2026 results, reporting a net loss of $19.1 million despite a 40% revenue increase. The sports streaming service also revealed plans for a relatively rare reverse stock split, which contributed to the sharp decline in its stock price. This comes about a year after Disney's agreement to acquire a 70% stake in the streamer.

Mattel’s American Girl and Disney Consumer Products Renew Multi-Year Licensing Agreement for Disney Princess and Disney Frozen Franchises. Mattel's American Girl and Disney Consumer Products have renewed their multi-year licensing agreement. This continuation focuses on the popular Disney Princess and Disney Frozen franchises.

Disney parks chief Josh D’Amaro will take over for Bob Iger as CEO. The Walt Disney Company has named Josh D’Amaro, its parks chief, as the new CEO, succeeding Bob Iger. D'Amaro, who has overseen Disney's Experiences division since 2020, will lead the company at a time of strong box-office performance and streaming growth but also a decline in foreign theme park visitors. Dana Walden, co-chairman of Disney Entertainment, will transition to the newly created role of president and chief creative officer, reporting to D'Amaro.

Josh D’Amaro Named Next CEO of The Walt Disney Company. Josh D’Amaro, head of Disney Experiences and a 28-year veteran of the company, has been unanimously approved by The Walt Disney Company Board of Directors to be the next Chief Executive Officer. He will officially assume the role from Bob Iger at the Annual Meeting on March 18, 2026, and is also expected to join the Disney Board. Dana Walden will be appointed President and Chief Creative Officer on the same day.

Disney board taps Josh D’Amaro to succeed retiring CEO Bob Iger. Josh D'Amaro, former president of Walt Disney World and current chairman of Disney Experiences, has been unanimously selected by the Walt Disney Co. board of directors to succeed Bob Iger as CEO, effective March 18. D'Amaro, a 28-year Disney veteran, will take over leadership of the company, which includes theme parks, cruise lines, and media entities, with an annual base salary of $2.5 million. Bob Iger will remain on the board as a senior adviser until his second retirement on December 31.
